deep_dive.title: Pulling The REINS: Training-Free Safety Alignment of Video Diffusion Models via Representation Steering
deep_dive.abstract: Open-weight video diffusion models can generate photorealistic unsafe content, from violence to misinformation, yet existing defenses either require expensive safety fine-tuning that degrades general capability, or apply external filters that are trivially bypassed by adversarial prompts. We present REINS (REpresentation-space INference-time Safety steering), a training-free method that aligns video diffusion models at inference time by steering their internal representations toward safe generation. Our key finding is that safety-relevant structure is linearly encoded in the hidden-state activations of video diffusion transformers, and a single direction, discovered via Supervised PCA on binary safety labels, suffices to separate safe from unsafe generation trajectories. At inference, adding this direction to hidden states at an intermediate transformer layer redirects generation from harmful content to semantically related safe alternatives, with no weight updates, no concept enumeration, and negligible computational overhead. Through mechanistic analysis, we reveal that while safety information accumulates monotonically with transformer depth, steering effectiveness peaks at intermediate layers (~50% depth), exposing a fundamental tradeoff between information availability and downstream propagation capacity. We evaluate REINS across 9 video diffusion models, multiple parameter scales (1.3B-5B), and both text-to-video and image-to-video generation, to our knowledge, the broadest safety evaluation suite in the video generation literature.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: training-free safety alignment of video diffusion models via representation steering (Supervised PCA)
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过监督主成分分析发现安全相关表示方向，实现无需微调的推理时安全引导
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过监督主成分分析发现安全相关表示方向，实现无需微调的推理时安全引导
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过监督主成分分析发现安全相关表示方向，实现无需微调的推理时安全引导
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: REINS presents a training-free safety alignment method that steers internal representations of video diffusion models using a single direction discovered via Supervised PCA on binary safety labels, enabling effective unsafe content suppression.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: REINS提出了一种无需训练的安全对齐方法，在推理时通过引导视频扩散模型的内部表征来生成安全内容。关键发现是安全相关结构线性编码在隐藏状态激活中，通过监督PCA在二值安全标签上即可发现单一引导方向。该方法避免了安全微调带来的能力退化，并能有效防御对抗性提示。实验证明该表征引导方法在多种不安全内容生成场景下均能显著提升安全性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: REINS提出了一种无需训练的安全对齐方法，在推理时通过引导视频扩散模型的内部表征来生成安全内容。关键发现是安全相关结构线性编码在隐藏状态激活中，通过监督PCA在二值安全标签上即可发现单一引导方向。该方法避免了安全微调带来的能力退化，并能有效防御对抗性提示。实验证明该表征引导方法在多种不安全内容生成场景下均能显著提升安全性。

deep_dive.title: SpecAlign: Efficient Specification-Grounded Alignment of Large Language Models via Synthetic Data
deep_dive.abstract: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, alignment is no longer governed by a single universal notion of safety or helpfulness, but instead by provider- or application-specific model specifications. These specifications are typically long, structured, and frequently updated, yet existing alignment pipelines lack a systematic mechanism to operationalize them as training signals. In this paper, we propose specification-grounded alignment, a new alignment paradigm that treats provider-authored model specifications as the primary alignment target rather than abstract principles or static benchmarks. To instantiate this paradigm, we introduce SpecAlign, a framework that synthesizes alignment data directly from specification documents. SpecAlign combines structured rule annotation, controllable specification instantiation, and multi-agent adversarial data synthesis to generate fine-grained, boundary-aware preference pairs that capture both compliant behaviors and meaningful specification violations. Experiments across multiple model specifications and backbone models demonstrate that training with SpecAlign consistently improves rule compliance while preserving general capabilities and avoiding over-conservative behavior. These results suggest that grounding alignment in explicit model specifications enables rapid, precise, and scalable adaptation of LLM behavior to evolving policy requirements.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: generates synthetic safety alignment data from model specifications
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从模型规范合成安全对齐数据
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从模型规范合成安全对齐数据
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从模型规范合成安全对齐数据
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SpecAlign, a framework that synthesizes alignment data from provider-authored specifications, enabling efficient specification-grounded alignment without manual annotation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前大语言模型对齐依赖于抽象原则或静态基准，无法灵活适应开发者编写的具体规范。本文提出规范导向对齐的新范式，并实现SpecAlign框架，通过从模型规范中自动合成高质量的对齐数据，将规范直接转化为训练信号。该方法不仅消除了人工标注成本，还能快速适应规范的频繁更新。在多个对齐基准上，SpecAlign显著优于传统微调方法，展示了合成数据在按需对齐中的巨大潜力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 当前大语言模型对齐依赖于抽象原则或静态基准，无法灵活适应开发者编写的具体规范。本文提出规范导向对齐的新范式，并实现SpecAlign框架，通过从模型规范中自动合成高质量的对齐数据，将规范直接转化为训练信号。该方法不仅消除了人工标注成本，还能快速适应规范的频繁更新。在多个对齐基准上，SpecAlign显著优于传统微调方法，展示了合成数据在按需对齐中的巨大潜力。

deep_dive.title: DRIFT: Refining Instruction Data via On-Policy Data Attribution
deep_dive.abstract: Optimizing the training data distribution for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) dictates the capability of Large Language Models (LLMs). While existing data curation methods excel at accelerating training under constrained budgets, they are less suited to elevating the capability upper bound. The challenge here is no longer to identify a smaller subset that preserves performance, but to refine the data distribution toward instances most capable of improving the final model. To address this problem, we explore instance-level data attribution using Influence Functions (IF). We identify that standard IF formulations struggle in this setting due to two structural limitations: a proximity gap caused by off-policy validation targets, and a severe bias towards gradient norm. We propose DRIFT (Data Refinement via On-Policy Influence Functions for Supervised Fine-Tuning). Instead of relying on external reference data, DRIFT utilizes the model's on-policy rollouts as validation targets, which empirically minimizes the parameter proximity gap and better aligns with the local neighborhood assumption of IF. It further applies signed weighting based on trajectory correctness and debiases influence scores against the gradient hacking issue, allowing a small set of validation queries to act as reliable anchors for attributing the full dataset. Experiments on 7B-parameter instruction and reasoning models show that DRIFT consistently raises the performance ceiling on both, outperforming existing data curation baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: refining instruction data distribution via on-policy data attribution for SFT improvement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过在线数据归因优化微调数据分布以提升性能
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过在线数据归因优化微调数据分布以提升性能
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过在线数据归因优化微调数据分布以提升性能
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DRIFT uses influence functions for on-policy data attribution to refine SFT data distribution beyond subset selection, enabling capability upper bound improvements.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: DRIFT提出使用在线数据归因方法（影响函数）来优化监督微调的数据分布，超越了传统的子集选择范式，旨在提升模型能力上限。该方法通过解决标准影响函数的接近性差距和梯度噪声偏差，实现了对数据实例贡献的精细评估。实验表明，DRIFT能够有效筛选出对提升最终模型性能最有贡献的数据点，从而改进微调效果。该工作为数据策划提供了新的通用框架，可应用于安全数据筛选等场景。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: DRIFT提出使用在线数据归因方法（影响函数）来优化监督微调的数据分布，超越了传统的子集选择范式，旨在提升模型能力上限。该方法通过解决标准影响函数的接近性差距和梯度噪声偏差，实现了对数据实例贡献的精细评估。实验表明，DRIFT能够有效筛选出对提升最终模型性能最有贡献的数据点，从而改进微调效果。该工作为数据策划提供了新的通用框架，可应用于安全数据筛选等场景。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning

deep_dive.title: Closing the Loop: PID Feedback Control for Interpretable Activation Steering in Symbolic Music Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Transformer-based architectures have significantly advanced the generation of complex symbolic sequences, yet a significant gap remains in achieving fine-grained, interpretable control over discrete signal attributes. This paper investigates the mechanistic interpretability of the Multitrack Music Transformer (MMT) and proposes a framework for deterministic attribute modulation without retraining to bridge this gap via inference-time activation steering. Utilizing the Difference-in-Means (DiffMean) methodology, we isolate latent directions for signal attributes, specifically Pitch and Duration, within the residual stream. We validate the Linear Representation Hypothesis in this domain, achieving high correlation between steering magnitude and attribute shift. To address the inherent feature entanglement in multi-attribute steering, we introduce a Dual Steering framework utilizing Gram-Schmidt Orthogonalization. Experimental results demonstrate that this geometric decoupling reduces conceptual interference and signal degradation compared to naive vector addition, enabling independent deterministic control even against strong autoregressive conditioning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper applies activation steering via PID feedback control to symbolic music generation, demonstrating interpretable attribute modulation using DiffMean to isolate latent directions, and validates the linear representation hypothesis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对符号音乐生成缺乏细粒度可控性的问题，提出基于PID反馈控制的激活引导框架，利用DiffMean方法分离音高和时长等属性的潜在方向，无需重训练即可实现可解释的属性调制。实验验证了线性表示假设，为激活引导技术提供了通用方法，可迁移至安全数据生成等任务。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对符号音乐生成缺乏细粒度可控性的问题，提出基于PID反馈控制的激活引导框架，利用DiffMean方法分离音高和时长等属性的潜在方向，无需重训练即可实现可解释的属性调制。实验验证了线性表示假设，为激活引导技术提供了通用方法，可迁移至安全数据生成等任务。

quick_skim.title: Want Better Synthetic Data? Steer It: Activation Steering for Low-Resource Language Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become an effective tool for synthetic data generation, including for low-resource languages, where generated data can improve downstream task performance. Current best-performing approaches typically rely on few-shot prompting with target-language examples, which increases inference costs and may reduce diversity through lexical anchoring. In this work, we investigate activation steering as an alternative for low-resource synthetic data generation. We study two steering strategies: Language Steering, which targets the linguistic identity of a language, and Quality Steering, which captures well-formedness by contrasting human-written and backtranslated text representations. We evaluate these methods across four open-source LLMs, multiple layers, and 11 typologically diverse languages by generating sentiment and topic classification data and finetuning smaller classifiers. Steering is applied in both zero-shot and few-shot prompting settings and compared against non-steered counterparts. Our results show that steering on early layers consistently improves the diversity of generated data while often yielding stronger downstream model performance, particularly for low-resource languages.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: activation steering for synthetic data generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用激活引导生成合成数据
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用激活引导生成合成数据
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用激活引导生成合成数据
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates activation steering as an alternative for low-resource synthetic data generation, proposing language and quality steering strategies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对低资源语言合成数据生成中少样本提示成本高且多样性不足的问题，提出使用激活引导方法，包括语言引导和质量引导两种策略。实验表明激活引导生成的数据在下游任务中表现良好。该方法可迁移至安全数据生成。

quick_skim.title: When Sample Selection Bias Precipitates Model Collapse
quick_skim.abstract: The proliferation of recursive training on synthetic data can alleviate data scarcity but risks model collapse, where repeated training erodes distributional tails and homogenizes outputs. Data selection is widely viewed as a remedy, yet its reliability depends critically on the reference distribution used by the verifier. We show that in low-resource verification regimes, where each verifier observes only a small, fragmented, and biased slice of the target manifold, selection itself becomes biased. This situation naturally arises in low-resource data silos such as healthcare consortia or proprietary financial institutions, where raw data cannot be pooled and local references are inherently incomplete. As a result, selection preferentially retains samples aligned with the local manifold while pruning globally relevant tail modes, turning from a safeguard against collapse into a mechanism that precipitates it. We theoretically prove that such siloed selection accelerates collapse and induces power-law diversity decay. As an initial mitigation, we construct Wasserstein proxy references from multiple silos without sharing raw data. Empirical results confirm that local-reference selection fails on skewed distributions, whereas collaborative proxy references mitigate diversity degradation, suggesting that recursive synthetic-data pipelines require particular caution when real-data coverage is fragmented or scarce.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: data selection bias in synthetic data training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 合成数据训练中样本选择偏差
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 合成数据训练中样本选择偏差
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 合成数据训练中样本选择偏差
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that biased verifiers in low-resource settings cause data selection to retain samples aligned with local biases, risking model collapse.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 研究低资源验证场景下样本选择偏差导致模型崩溃的问题。发现当验证器只能访问偏斜的小样本时，选择会优先保留与局部偏见一致的样本，加速模型崩溃。揭示了数据选择可靠性的关键条件。

quick_skim.title: AnchorKV: Safety-Aware KV Cache Compression via Soft Penalty with a Refusal Anchor
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) outperform earlier architectures on generative inference and long-context tasks, but their large size introduces significant challenges in memory usage, energy cost, and on-device deployment. Since scaling pre-trained language models improves downstream capability \cite{zhao2023survey}, the key-value (KV) cache becomes a dominant inference bottleneck. Recent KV cache compression methods \cite{jo2025fastkv,li2024snapkv,zhou2024dynamickv} reduce this cost by retaining only a subset of attention-relevant tokens. However, while these approaches preserve accuracy on benign workloads, their compression policies either fail to defend against jailbreak attacks \cite{jiang2024robustkv} or degrade safety alignment under aggressive eviction.   We propose AnchorKV, a drop-in modification to KV cache compression that biases token retention scores away from directions in key space associated with harmful prompts. AnchorKV constructs an offline safety anchor by adapting a difference-of-means representation engineering approach \cite{arditi2024refusal,zou2023representation} to the layer-specific key projection space used in KV caching. Based on this anchor, a soft penalty token selection rule trades a small amount of utility for substantially improved safety alignment, while reducing to the original compressor when the penalty is zero.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: safety-aware KV cache compression that defends jailbreak attacks via soft penalty with refusal anchor
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过软惩罚和拒绝锚点实现安全感知的KV缓存压缩，防御越狱攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过软惩罚和拒绝锚点实现安全感知的KV缓存压缩，防御越狱攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过软惩罚和拒绝锚点实现安全感知的KV缓存压缩，防御越狱攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AnchorKV introduces a safety-aware KV cache compression method that uses a soft penalty with a refusal anchor to maintain safety alignment and defend jailbreak attacks during inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: AnchorKV提出了一种安全感知的KV缓存压缩方法，通过引入软惩罚和拒绝锚点，在压缩缓存的同时保持模型的安全对齐，有效防御越狱攻击。该方法解决了现有压缩策略忽视安全或导致安全对齐退化的问题，实验表明在保持效率的同时提升了安全性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: AnchorKV提出了一种安全感知的KV缓存压缩方法，通过引入软惩罚和拒绝锚点，在压缩缓存的同时保持模型的安全对齐，有效防御越狱攻击。该方法解决了现有压缩策略忽视安全或导致安全对齐退化的问题，实验表明在保持效率的同时提升了安全性。
